summary: Rule chunk from Paranoia for Players page 19, chunk 4.

mechanics.raw_text:
Worst of all, if you ever get 20 treason points, you will be terminated. Luckily, there's a way to reduce your treason point total: perform meritorious service for The Computer. Receiving commendations from your superiors, successfully completing mis- sions, unmasking and executing traitors, and being especially obsequious when talking, with The Computer are all likely to do the trick. Getting killed more or less wipes the slate clean for the next clone, too. TREASON DOES NOT PAY, SERVE THE COMPUTER 
, 9. Paranoia Etiquette 
Privacy Mary activities that would be unobjec- tionable in other roleplaying games are bad form in Paranoia. For example, looking. at another player's character sheet is an unfair ‘way to obtain evidence of teason agains him, Enesdropping_ on another ‘oubleshooter ‘while playing in characteris fine, but eaves ropping on a private conference between the tgamemaster and a player is cheating Please respect the privacy ofthe gamemaster and other players. Ignorance is an important ‘element inthe drama of a Paranoia adventure. Each player is entitled tothe confidentiality of his character, character records, and player GM communications 
Ultraviolet Information ‘Many games divide rules into “Player” in- formation and “Gamemaster” info. So does Paranoia. But Paranoia depends more than most on keeping the players in the dark. That's why much of this book is classified Ultraviolet
